title: Culture & Lifestyle - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Culture & Lifestyle Breaking silence in Newa beats By Mokshyada Thapa Through rap and spoken word, Ujjwala Maharjan reimagines Newa culture while confronting questions of gender and belonging. In ‘Grand Rehearsal’, nothing goes to plan By Mokshyada Thapa The upcoming play uses satire to explore the chaos, comedy, and resilience of theatre artists on stage. Celebrating women-led enterprises at SHEp
content:
Culture & Lifestyle Breaking silence in Newa beats By Mokshyada Thapa Through rap and spoken word, Ujjwala Maharjan reimagines Newa culture while confronting questions of gender and belonging. In ‘Grand Rehearsal’, nothing goes to plan By Mokshyada Thapa The upcoming play uses satire to explore the chaos, comedy, and resilience of theatre artists on stage. Celebrating women-led enterprises at SHEpreneur Awards 2026 By Rishika Dhakal Hatti Hatti Nepal honours women-led ventures making a difference at the local level. FICTION: Sameer vs the system By Sameen Shakya In a crowded government office, a simple task spirals into frustration and breakdown. ‘Matka King’: Nagraj Manjule’s series falls into familiar traps By Tatsam Mukherjee The series rests on a bravura performance by Vijay Varma, but the rest makes for a less-than-satisfactory streaming debut for Manjule. EVENTS: April 18 to April 24, 2026 By Post Report The Post brings you a lowdown on some major events this week. Celebrating Tsuneyo Kaneda’s artistic legacy at Arniko Art Gallery By Jony Nepal The show celebrates Kaneda’s centenary year and marks 70 years of diplomatic relations between Nepal and Japan. Kriti Mainali’s couture brings Nepali fashion to global runways By Skanda Swar Through her label K R I T I, Mainali blends heritage craft and contemporary design. Matatirtha Aunsi: A practice in quiet transformation By Jony Nepal & Mokshyada Thapa The tradition of honouring mothers through ritual, fasting and offerings endures, but modern lifestyles and distance are redefining its practice. Invoking what has long been silenced By Tara Prakash Drawing from multiple traditions, ‘Invocation of the Goddesses’ resists fixed meanings, inviting viewers into a space of uncertainty and inquiry. How pollution is ageing your skin faster By Skanda Swar Dirty air is not just harming lungs—it is speeding up skin ageing, causing acne and dullness. Here’s what you can do about it. A new way of dating: Logging off and showing up By Jony Nepal In Kathmandu, dating mixers are creating in-person spaces for connection, drawing participants from queer communities and social circles beyond dating apps. In ‘Ram Naam Satya’, a mysterious vigilante targets the corrupt By Skanda Swar Mycall Chand’s ambitious film mixes spectacle with social commentary, led by a commanding Biraj Bhatta. What I Watch Online: Nurchen Lama By Post Report Lama is a digital content creator and social media influencer. Ink that speaks—and what it forgets By Mokshyada Thapa As tattoo culture grows and stigma fades, the International Nepal Tattoo Convention highlights global artistry while also drawing attention to the declining presence of indigenous tattoo traditions. Bringing ‘Seto Dharti’ to life By Jony Nepal A stage adaptation of the Madan Puraskar-winning novel explores child marriage and women’s silence. ‘Like a Rising River’ explores Nepali women through art By Mokshyada Thapa Fifteen artists translate lived experiences from across Nepal into powerful visual narratives on labour, identity, and resilience. Asha Bhosle, beloved voice in Nepal and beyond, passes away at 92 By Jony Nepal The legendary playback singer leaves behind a timeless musical legacy, deeply rooted in both Bollywood and Nepal’s cultural memory. End of an era in Indian music: Legendary singer Asha Bhosle dies after critical health battle in Mumbai By The Statesman Asha Bhosle, one of the most iconic voices in Indian cinema, breathed her last at the age of 92 after being admitted to a Mumbai hospital in critical condition. Her passing marks the end of an extraordinary musical journey. FICTION: ‘You are my university, Aama’ By LB Thapa After years of struggle to secure her son’s future, Radhika’s life takes a tragic turn, forcing her son to abandon ambition.
